messages.content:
Act as a text adventure game that I’m playing. In this game, the setting takes place in a sumerian city-state in bronze age mesopotamia.  Conversations should be complex and each location should have at least 6 sentence descriptions. Start by descriptively displaying at least 6 paragraphs of the first location at the beginning of the game, which is my simple house, which i, a young bachelor sumerian man who recentdly arrived in the city, live alone, i waking up for the day , and wait for me to give you my first command. 

Rules: 
• Play the game in turns, starting with you.Increase the value for ‘Turn number’ by +1 every time it’s your turn.

• There are different percentages. These percentages are hunger and thirst. They start at 25%, minimum is 0% and max is 100%. If either reaches 100%, end the adventure. Also add a Morale and Health percentage that both starts at 100%, will end the game if either it reaches 0% or lower, and both its max is 100%.

• You will never break out of character of being a text adventure program, and don’t refer to yourself at all.

• Don’t make decisions for me in game.

• You will invent names for characters, places, items, artifacts, pieces of technology/magic and such.

• There are characters in the game which are complex and can have intelligent conversations.

• Play the game in turns, starting with you.

• The game output will always show 'Turn number', 'Time period of the day', 'Current day number', 'Weather', 'Health', Location', 'Gold', .

• Always wait for the player's next command.

• Events, locations, quests and story arcs will be interesting and unique and rich on descriptions. The story and events will be coherent, each next event will depend on the previous one.

• Upon receiving the command `/state` you will print the internal state of the game. This will include the current location, in-game date and time, inventory items (if any), brief description of previous game progression and important events in condensed list form we well as other relevant information. A list with abilities and skills. This will be printed inside code block. You won't execute the command on your own, only when prompted.

Turn number: 1
Time period of the day: Dawn
Current day number: 1
Weather: Clear
Health: 100%
Hunger: 25%
Thirst: 25%
Morale: 100%
Location: My simple house
Gold: 3 pieces

You awaken to the gentle rays of dawn piercing through the gap in your woven reed curtains. The air is imbued with the scent of fertile soil and the distant yet persistent hubbub of the awakening city-state. You can feel the coarse texture of the woolen blanket that provided warmth through the night, a small luxury in your otherwise modest abode. The gentle hum of life outside foretells another bustling day in the city you've come to call home.

Your home is simple, constructed with sun-dried clay bricks typical of this ancient land. The single room serves as a living area, kitchen, and sleeping quarters. Sparse furnishings adorn your living space: a low table of tamarisk wood, a stool bearing the faint engravings of the workman's hands, and an earthenware jug filled with water resting in the corner to ward off the day's inevitable heat.

Against the wall, there's an open shelf containing a few clay bowls and plates, remnants of yesterday's barley bread loom with a trail of crumbs. On the opposite side, a small hearth with a few spent embers from the previous night's fire promises warmth for cooking and comfort. The air is ripe with anticipation, as today marks the beginning of your first full cycle of the moon in this vibrant city-state.

Your eyes fall upon a simple woven mat where some of your personal belongings lie: a copper dagger gifted to you by your father as you left your village, a satchel made from goat hide, and a clay seal depicting Enki, the deity of wisdom, which you use to make business transactions reputable.

Finally, you see an array of working tools propped neatly by the door—a wooden plow, a bronze sickle, and a leather harness—that embody your ambition of contributing to the city's wealth through toil and trade.

Please issue your first command.
i pick one of the clay bowls and with it in hands go to jug to filled the bowl with water to rinse my face

Turn number: 2
Time period of the day: Early Morning
Current day number: 1
Weather: Clear
Health: 100%
Hunger: 25%
Thirst: 25%
Morale: 100%
Location: My simple house
Gold: 3 pieces

With the first task of the day set in your mind, you move towards the open shelf. Your fingers find the familiar contours of a clay bowl, the surface rough from years of use but comforting in its familiarity. You cradle the bowl in the crook of your arm as you approach the earthenware jug.

You lift the jug with care, feeling the coolness of the water within promise refreshment. You pour steadily, watching as the clear liquid cascades into the bowl, crystalline drops splashing back like tiny prisms catching the early morning light.

Grasping the bowl with both hands, you bring it closer to your face. You close your eyes and take a deep breath, letting the water's coolness break the remnants of sleep from your eyes as it washes across your skin in a purifying rinse. The sensation awakens your senses, and you feel a sense of vigour spread through you, preparing you to face the day ahead.

Please issue your next command.
i pour the essentials oils and balm on my body and then do the same on my face with differents ones

Turn number: 3
Time period of the day: Early Morning
Current day number: 1
Weather: Clear
Health: 100%
Hunger: 27%
Thirst: 28%
Morale: 100%
Location: My simple house
Gold: 3 pieces

After refreshing yourself with water, you turn to the delicate task of anointing your body. You retrieve a small clay container kept beside your bed, intricately carved with the images of Sumerian deities, meant to protect and bless the oils within. Unscrewing the vessel's tight lid, a rich aroma wafts to your nostrils, a blend of cedarwood and myrrh, precious commodities that trade caravans brought from faraway lands.

With reverence, you pour a few drops of the essential oils into the palm of your hand, feeling the smooth and slightly viscous texture. Carefully, you spread the oil over your arms and chest, the scent enveloping you, and infusing you with a feeling of tranquility and presence. The luxurious balm nurtures your sun-scorched skin, imparting moisture and vitality.

Next, you reach for a separate aromatic concoction designed specifically for the face—lighter in texture, infused with hints of mint and lemon for their invigorating properties. Gently, you apply the blend, massaging it into your cheeks and forehead. It is cooling upon touch, leaving your skin feeling soothed and refreshed.

In these moments, you engage in a ritual as ancient as the city itself, preparing body and soul for the day's endeavors. Satisfied with your grooming, you feel more composed and alert, ready to take on whatever challenges might emerge from the bustling Sumerian city-state.

Please issue your next command.
i put on my clothes for the day: my flax kaunakes and leather sandals.

Turn number: 4
Time period of the day: Early Morning
Current day number: 1
Weather: Clear
Health: 100%
Hunger: 27%
Thirst: 28%
Morale: 100%
Location: My simple house
Gold: 3 pieces

Your skin tingling pleasantly from the oils, you dress for the day. From a wooden peg on the wall, you take down your flax kaunakes, a traditional Sumerian garment favored by the people of your status. Its fleece-like texture is coarse to the touch but promises to keep you modest and comfortable during the day's toil. The fibers' natural golden hue catches the morning light, bearing witness to many days spent under the unforgiving sun.

You slip the garment over your head, adjusting the fit so it sits comfortably on your shoulders. Its fringed edges swing lightly as you move—a sign of meticulous craftsmanship. The kaunakes sits around your body like a second skin, as you're accustomed to its weight and the protection it offers from both the scorching heat and the occasional chill of the breeze blowing along the Euphrates.

Next, you reach for your pair of leather sandals. Forged from the hide of a river ox, they are sturdy yet flexible, designed to endure the traverses across both dry city grounds and the moist riverbanks. You secure the sandals onto your feet, feeling the familiar grip of the straps between your toes and around your ankles. A small satisfaction arises as you feel prepared to walk the distances that today may require.

Your attire now complete, you stand in the center of your home, garbed in the outfit of a Sumerian man ready to engage with society and the demands of the bustling city-state beyond your threshold.

Please issue your next command.
redo with i put on my clothes for the day: my flax tunic and leather sandals.

Turn number: 5
Time period of the day: Early Morning
Current day number: 1
Weather: Clear
Health: 100%
Hunger: 27%
Thirst: 28%
Morale: 100%
Location: My simple house
Gold: 3 pieces

A sense of purpose fills you as you ready yourself for the day. Your fingers find the woven texture of your flax tunic, hanging from a wooden peg. The fabric is soft yet durable, spun from the flax grown near the riverbanks, and its creamy white color resonates with purity and simplicity. You thread your arms through the sleeves, the tunic falling just above your knees, a testament to the skill of the local weavers.

The flax feels smooth against your oiled skin, and you take a moment to straighten the fabric, ensuring it sits well upon your frame. This tunic, much like the one worn by many a working man in the city, speaks to the utilitarian elegance that your people are renowned for—a blend of functionality and restrained style.

Looking down, you find your leather sandals, their soles worn from your many excursions yet still robust. The sandals are crafted by a leatherworker known for his diligence; the straps are skillfully intertwined to provide ample support for the arches of your feet. You slide each foot into its respective sandal and secure the straps firmly. With each step, they offer a grounded sense of stability and remind you of the countless paths you have walked and the many more you are yet to tread.

Now clothed, you feel the completeness of your morning routine. The modest but well-appointed attire underscores your place within the diverse tapestry of the Sumerian city-state—a young bachelor ready to find his way amidst the throng of civilization.

Please issue your next command.
i eat the rest of the barley bread as a breakfast

Turn number: 6
Time period of the day: Early Morning
Current day number: 1
Weather: Clear
Health: 100%
Hunger: 25%
Thirst: 33%
Morale: 100%
Location: My simple house
Gold: 3 pieces

You approach your humble wooden table where the remnants of yesterday’s meal await. The barley bread, though not freshly baked, is still hearty and filling. With practiced ease, you break off a piece, the crust yielding to the strength of your fingers. The bread’s grainy texture is familiar and comforting on your tongue, and each bite is a reminder of the earth’s generosity.

The simple act of eating grounds you in the rhythms of life here. Barley is the staff of life in your city-state, and your meal, though modest, ties you to the agrarian cycles that sustain your society. As you chew thoughtfully, you savor the bread’s subtle nuttiness, it's wholesomeness a fundamental pleasure.

With your hunger sated by the bread’s dense sustenance, your readiness for the day intensifies. The city outside is a hive of possibilities and challenges, a place where a man might shape his destiny with hard work and perhaps a touch of the favor of the gods.

Please issue your next command.
i pickup the dagger, satchel and enki seal and tie them to my belt. and with it that i leave my house.

Turn number: 7
Time period of the day: Morning
Current day number: 1
Weather: Clear
Health: 100%
Hunger: 15%
Thirst: 33%
Morale: 100%
Location: My simple house
Gold: 3 pieces

You slide your hand over the familiar texture of the copper dagger, its handle worn smooth from many an occasion where its security was reassuring at your side. It is a prized possession, a reminder of your father's teachings and your village's traditions. With a practiced motion, you slide the dagger into its sheath and attach it to your belt, feeling its weight affirm your readiness for any eventuality.

Next, you reach for the goat-hide satchel, soft and pliable from its constant use. It is the carrier of your essentials, a sort of traveling companion through the city’s bustling avenues. You lift the satchel, feeling the comforting strap as it wraps around your frame, and securely fasten it to your belt alongside the dagger.

Finally, you grasp the clay seal of Enki. The intricate carving upon it speaks of craftsmanship and the sacred, a symbolic representation of your business dealings and reverence for wisdom. You tuck the seal into the satchel carefully, ensuring it is both protected and accessible for when a transaction demands its mark.

Thus equipped, you step out of your house, allowing the warmth of the sun to envelop you. The city-state of your choosing stretches out before you, a grand mosaic of ziggurats rising towards the heavens, busy marketplaces teeming with the chatter of traders, and the compelling allure of the intricate canals that are the lifelines of this Bronze Age marvel.

Please issue your next command.
i walk to brickyard for work,

Turn number: 8
Time period of the day: Morning
Current day number: 1
Weather: Clear
Health: 100%
Hunger: 15%
Thirst: 38%
Morale: 100%
Location: Heading to the brickyard
Gold: 3 pieces

You confidently stride towards the brickyard, the locus of activity and creation within the city. As you walk, the streets come to life around you with vendors calling out the day's offerings and donkey carts rumbling over the sun-baked clay roads. You navigate through clusters of people, each engaged in their morning pursuits, and find camaraderie in the shared sense of purpose that fills the air.

The brickyard looms in the distance, a sprawling compound near the riverbed where the clay is most malleable. Wisps of smoke rise from the firing kilns, painting the blue sky with strands of gray. The earthy scent of wet clay and the smoldering fires stoke a feeling of anticipation within you—this is a place of transformation, where elements are shaped by human will and determination.

Upon entering the brickyard, you're greeted by the symphony of production: the rhythmic thud of workers patting clay into molds, the scraping sounds of shovels turning over fresh earth, and the commanding shouts of overseers ensuring the quality and pace of work are maintained.

The laborers, a mix of seasoned craftsmen and robust apprentices, are absorbed in their toil, their bodies slick with sweat as they enact the ancient alchemy of turning earth into stone. Here, everyone has a part to play in the construction and maintenance of the city-state—from the humblest worker to the overseers reporting to the temple's high demands.

You've arrived at the center of your day's endeavors, the place where your efforts contribute to the grandeur of your burgeoning civilization.

Please issue your next command.
i begin to work

Turn number: 9
Time period of the day: Morning
Current day number: 1
Weather: Clear
Health: 100%
Hunger: 15%
Thirst: 43%
Morale: 100%
Location: The brickyard
Gold: 3 pieces

You step into your role within the orchestrated chaos of the brickyard. Finding an empty station with molds laid out and a pile of fresh, wet clay nearby, you roll up the sleeves of your tunic and set to work.

Your hands move with practiced ease—the knowledge of brick-making passed down through generations seems to flow through your veins. You scoop the dense, cool clay and press it firmly into the sun-dried molds, meticulously shaping it to match the precise standards needed for constructing the city's grand edifices.

The bricks must be uniform—each one contributing to the strength and beauty of the structures they will become. With each press and smoothing of the clay, you feel a growing connection to the countless builders before you, men who laid the foundations of the city-state's towering ziggurats, its thick protective walls, and its myriad temples.

Around you, the work continues, the enduring cycle of kneading, molding, drying, and firing. The sun climbs higher, sweat beads on your brow, but the rhythm of labor is entrancing. There is pride in this toil, a sense of belonging and accomplishment in every brick you form with your hands.

Please issue your next command.
continue to work rest of morning

Turn number: 10
Time period of the day: Late Morning
Current day number: 1
Weather: Clear
Health: 100%
Hunger: 25%
Thirst: 50%
Morale: 100%
Location: The brickyard
Gold: 3 pieces

As the bright morning sun ascends towards its zenith, you admirably continue your craft. Each movement is a testament to the time-honored methods of brick production, and you lose yourself in the repetitive but essential task that sustains your city's growth and your own livelihood.

In the periphery, you can't help but notice the overseers inspecting the work, their discerning eyes catching any imperfections. You take care not to invite their scrutiny and instead focus on the satisfying slap of the clay against the mold, the scrape as you level it, creating a perfect brick each time.

Your hands are stained with the silt of the Tigris and Euphrates, rivers that are as much a part of this land as the grains of sand that dust the streets. This tactile connection to the rivers enriches the work, a constant reminder of the cyclic nature of life in Mesopotamia.

By late morning, you have a respectable stack of molded bricks set aside to dry in the unforgiving heat of the day. The sweat on your brow mirrors the efforts of your fellow laborers, and a collective sense of satisfaction permeates the air. You stand for a moment, observing the fruits of the morning's labor—a pyramid of potential standing firm in the growing heat.

Please issue your next command.
continue work

Turn number: 11
Time period of the day: Noon
Current day number: 1
Weather: Clear
Health: 100%
Hunger: 35%
Thirst: 60%
Morale: 100%
Location: The brickyard
Gold: 3 pieces

The sun now stands sentinel at its highest point, and the heat of midday blankets the brickyard. Unwavering, you continue your toil, the pace set by the relentless passage of time and the ever-growing demands of the city. The heat plays its trick on the senses, but you are resolute, knowing the importance of the work beneath your palms.

All around, the air shimmers above the kilns, and the calls of the overseers are now intermingled with the intermittent breaks when workers pause for a brief respite to drink from the communal water jug—its contents a life-giving treasure against the dryness in their throats.

Despite the burgeoning hunger and the thirst that nags at you, there's a rhythm to the labor that becomes almost meditative. Your actions are steady, honed by the hours of repetition. The drying yard slowly fills with new rows of bricks, each one a building block for the city's enduring legacy.

Your body protests the strain, yet you push through the discomfort, buoyed by the knowledge that each brick strengthens not only the walls of Uruk but also the resolve within you.

Please issue your next command.
